name: PyPI Insiders
cost: Free (open source, ISC license). Note: it exists to consume "Insiders"/sponsorware projects, which themselves require a paid sponsorship to access; the tool itself is free.
perf_notes: No published benchmarks. It is a thin CLI orchestrator around pypiserver (a single-process bottle/WSGI app) running on default port 31411, plus a polling watcher (default 30-min sleep) that runs git clone + python -m build + twine upload. pypiserver scans a flat directory and offers optional watchdog-based directory caching for "thousands of packages"; throughput is whatever the single bottle process plus the chosen WSGI server delivers. Designed for a small set of private Insiders packages, not high request volume.
scale_notes: Single-node, single-process by design. The bundled pypiserver runs locally (localhost:31411) with the watcher as a separate daemon process managed via CLI (server/watcher start|status|stop). No HA, clustering, multi-node, or stateless horizontal scaling story. State is files on the local filesystem (user_data_dir for dists, user_cache_dir for repo clones, repos.json config). pypiserver itself can sit behind nginx/traefik/gunicorn, but pypi-insiders does not provide any of that.
summary: A self-hosted, pypiserver-backed local index that watches sponsorware "Insiders" GitHub repos and automatically builds and uploads their private releases, giving local Insiders versions precedence over PyPI.
extras: Core value-add is automation, not the server: a watcher daemon polls configured GitHub 'Insiders' repos and auto-runs git clone + python -m build + twine upload on new versions
Bundles and shells out to pypiserver under the hood (dependency pypiserver>=1.5; pulls 2.4.x today) launched with auth fully disabled (-a. -P.) on default port 31411
Local index gives precedence to local Insiders packages and 302-redirects unknown names to PyPI (pypiserver --fallback-url default), so public deps still resolve
Can upload built dists to ANY index, including remote private indexes that require auth, via --index-url/--index-user/--index-password (twine)
CLI process management: pypi-insiders server|watcher start/status/stop/logs; repos add/list/remove; update
Watched repos configured in repos.json using NAMESPACE/PROJECT:DISTRIBUTION_NAME mapping; repos cloned via SSH (git@github.com), relying on the user's SSH keys
Documents driving consuming clients by setting PIP_INDEX_URL / UV_INDEX_URL / PDM_PYPI_URL env vars
pypiserver exposes a minimal /<project>/json info endpoint and an XML-RPC /RPC2 search method (legacy pip search), plus a default /health endpoint and sha256 link-hash fragments
